data.content: Your code accesses `fileSystem.File.ReadAllText(...)`, `fileSystem.Directory.Exists(...)`, etc. through sub-objects. A common pattern is a flattened wrapper that simplifies this. Let me generate one:
data.reasoningText:
The user has a simple codebase using `System.IO.Abstractions` and its `IFileSystem` interface. They want a "file system wrapper" - this likely means they want a concrete implementation/wrapper class that wraps the real `System.IO.FileSystem` and implements `IFileSystem`, or perhaps they want a wrapper/helper class around `IFileSystem` that provides convenience methods.

Actually, wait - `System.IO.Abstractions` already provides `FileSystem` as the concrete implementation of `IFileSystem`. So what would a "wrapper" mean here?

Looking at this more carefully, the user says "My code already uses IFileSystem from System.IO.Abstractions everywhere. Can you generate a file system wrapper for me?" 
 
I'm thinking they might want a custom wrapper that simplifies the interface with higher-level convenience methods, or perhaps a testable wrapper for mocking purposes rather than using the built-in `FileSystem` directly. I'm realizing they probably want a simpler wrapper that flattens the `IFileSystem` interface—something that lets them call methods directly like `wrapper.ReadAllText()` instead of navigating through `fileSystem.File.ReadAllText()`. The question is whether this should add extra functionality like logging or retry logic, or just be a straightforward delegation layer. I'll go ahead and create a straightforward wrapper that exposes the most commonly used file system operations in a flattened interface, making it easier for services to consume.

toolCalls.name: create
create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-0e7254187e6e4b9c91212afb71df83f6/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs b/tmp/sv-0e7254187e6e4b9c91212afb71df83f6/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-0e7254187e6e4b9c91212afb71df83f6/Services/FileSystemWrapper.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,88 @@
+using System.IO.Abstractions;
+
+namespace MyApp.Services;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// Delegates every call to the underlying <see cref="IFileSystem"/> from System.IO.Abstractions.
+/// </summary>
+public class FileSystemWrapper(IFileSystem fileSystem) : IFileSystemWrapper
+{
+    // File operations
+
+    public bool FileExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Exists(path);
+
+    public string ReadAllText(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllText(path);
+
+    public byte[] ReadAllBytes(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllBytes(path);
+
+    public string[] ReadAllLines(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllLines(path);
+
+    public void WriteAllText(string path, string contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllText(path, contents);
+
+    public void WriteAllBytes(string path, byte[] bytes) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllBytes(path, bytes);
+
+    public void WriteAllLines(string path, string[] contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllLines(path, contents);
+
+    public void CopyFile(string sourceFileName, string destFileName, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Copy(sourceFileName, destFileName, overwrite);
+
+    public void MoveFile(string sourceFileName, string destFileName) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Move(sourceFileName, destFileName);
+
+    public void DeleteFile(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Delete(path);
+
+    public Stream OpenRead(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.OpenRead(path);
+
+    public Stream Create(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Create(path);
+
+    // Directory operations
+
+    public bool DirectoryExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Exists(path);
+
+    public void CreateDirectory(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.CreateDirectory(path);
+
+    public void DeleteDirectory(string path, bool recursive = false) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Delete(path, recursive);
+
+    public string[] GetFiles(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetFiles(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    public string[] GetDirectories(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetDirectories(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    // Path operations
+
+    public string Combine(params string[] paths) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.Combine(paths);
+
+    public string GetDirectoryName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetDirectoryName(path)!;
+
+    public string GetFileName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileName(path);
+
+    public string GetFileNameWithoutExtension(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension(path);
+
+    public string GetExtension(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetExtension(path);
+
+    public string GetFullPath(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFullPath(path);
+
+    public string GetTempPath() =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetTempPath();
+}
+

+
+    public void CopyFile(string sourceFileName, string destFileName, bool overwrite = false) =>

+
+    public void MoveFile(string sourceFileName, string destFileName) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Move(sourceFileName, destFileName);
+
+    public void DeleteFile(string path) =>

+
+    public Stream OpenRead(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.OpenRead(path);
+
+    public Stream Create(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Create(path);
+
+    // Directory operations

+
+    public void CreateDirectory(string path) =>

+

+        fileSystem.Directory.Delete(path, recursive);
+
+    public string[] GetFiles(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetFiles(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    public string[] GetDirectories(string path, string searchPattern = "*", SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetDirectories(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    // Path operations
+
+    public string Combine(params string[] paths) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.Combine(paths);
+
+    public string GetDirectoryName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetDirectoryName(path)!;
+
+    public string GetFileName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileName(path);
+
+    public string GetFileNameWithoutExtension(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileNameWithoutExtension(path);
+
+    public string GetExtension(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetExtension(path);
+
+    public string GetFullPath(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFullPath(path);
+
+    public string GetTempPath() =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetTempPath();
+}
+

data.content:
Created two files:

- **`IFileSystemWrapper.cs`** — flattened interface covering common File, Directory, and Path operations.
- **`FileSystemWrapper.cs`** — implementation that delegates to `IFileSystem` via primary constructor injection.

Instead of `fileSystem.File.ReadAllText(path)`, your services can now use `wrapper.ReadAllText(path)`. Register it in DI like:

```csharp
services.AddSingleton<IFileSystemWrapper, FileSystemWrapper>();
```

For tests, mock `IFileSystemWrapper` directly — no need to set up the nested `IFileSystem` sub-objects.
